title: Conjecture 3 (List Hadwiger Conjecture)
statement_text: There exists an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that every $K_t$-minor free graph $G$ satisfies $\chi_\ell(G) \leq ct$.
context_text: The List Hadwiger Conjecture was first stated by Kawarabayashi and Mohar in 2007 and also appears in the Open Problem Garden. It asserts that the list chromatic number of $K_t$-minor free graphs is linearly bounded in $t$, as an asymptotic substitute for the exact version of Hadwiger's conjecture which fails for list coloring.
paper_contribution: Shows that $c \geq 2$ is necessary by constructing $K_t$-minor free graphs with list chromatic number at least $(2-o(1))t$ via the probabilistic method, improving the previous lower bound $c \geq \frac{4}{3}$ of Barát, Joret and Wood (2011).
paper_title: Improved lower bound for the list chromatic number of graphs with no $K_t$ minor

title: Conjecture 4 (Kawarabayashi-Mohar, $c = \tfrac{3}{2}$)
context_text: Kawarabayashi and Mohar stated their belief that the List Hadwiger Conjecture holds with the specific constant $c = \frac{3}{2}$; this also appears as Conjecture 8.4 in Seymour's survey on Hadwiger's conjecture. It strengthens the general linear conjecture by pinning down the constant.
paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by showing that the maximum list chromatic number of $K_t$-minor free graphs is at least $(2-o(1))t$, which strictly exceeds $\frac{3}{2}t$ for all sufficiently large $t$.
paper_title: Improved lower bound for the list chromatic number of graphs with no $K_t$ minor

title: Problem 1
context_text: Having established the lower bound $(2-o(1))t$ for the maximum list chromatic number of $K_t$-minor free graphs, the author asks whether the probabilistic lower-bound construction is essentially optimal up to lower-order terms, or whether the lower bound can be further improved beyond $2t$.
paper_title: Improved lower bound for the list chromatic number of graphs with no $K_t$ minor
